description: Color photograph (1200x855) of a CBP seizure of counterfeit and trademark-infringing luxury goods, displayed in what appears to be a warehouse or evidence-staging area. The frame is densely packed with piled designer merchandise atop cardboard boxes: handbags, sneakers, slides and sandals, and apparel bearing the logos and monograms of luxury brands. Visible items include Chanel-style quilted bags and a blanket with a large numeral '5' (Chanel No. 5 reference), a Louis Vuitton monogram tote and a small white tee with the LV monogram, a gray sweatshirt printed 'GIVENCHY PARIS', a Versace-style Medusa-motif gold piece, Christian Dior boxes, a red Yves Saint Laurent (YSL) bag, Fendi-style monogram items, and assorted Nike-style sneakers. There is no added graphic-design overlay, headline, or agency branding on the image itself. The accompanying tweet references counterfeiting and holiday shopping and includes #FakeGoodsRealDangers.
notable_text: (on-image brand text only: GIVENCHY PARIS, VENCHY, CHANEL '5', LV monogram, DIOR, FENDI ROMA) - no agency slogan on image
